questions.question: Why is a thread called a 'lightweight process'? (2 marks)
expectedPoints: Thread creation is faster than process creation as it shares existing process resources (memory, files).
Context switching between threads is faster than between processes as it involves saving less state (no MMU changes).

questions.question: Compare the Many-to-One and One-to-One multithreading models. Discuss one major advantage and one major disadvantage of each. (5 marks)
expectedPoints: Many-to-One Model: Advantage - Efficient thread management in user space, very fast. Disadvantage - Lack of true parallelism; a blocking call blocks all threads.

One-to-One Model: Advantage - Provides true parallelism and better responsiveness (blocking calls don't block entire process). Disadvantage - Overhead of creating a kernel thread for each user thread, may limit number of threads.
Conclusion: One-to-One is more common in modern OSs (Windows, Linux) despite overhead, due to the need for parallelism.
